hardened_backend_telemetry.p99_latency_profile: Stabilized under 1.2s via asyncio timeout guards

enforced_api_contracts: Global error boundaries emit bracketed custom exception logs: [VALIDATION_ERROR] and [TENANT_AUTH_ERROR]
POST /api/v1/procurement/ingest-history accepts multi-part form files and stages validated records in PostgreSQL
Track 1 gateway throttles emit [RATE_LIMIT_EXHAUSTED] at HTTP 429 for exhausted tenant or session windows

production_freeze.phase_registry: Phase 1 through Phase 8, Phase 11, and Deployment Track marked 100% COMPLETED
production_freeze.dead_code_sweep: core infra web/src raw print and console tracer scan clean
build_gates: python3 -m compileall main.py core/ infra/ scripts/ passed with zero warning or error trace
cd web && npm run build passed with zero warning or error trace

validation: Reviewed mandatory .antigravity guardrails, core engineering role constraints, and infra/database.py before mutation.
Added DATABASE_URL prefix normalization for postgresql://, postgres://, and postgresql+asyncpg:// before create_async_engine().
Translated sslmode query values into asyncpg-compatible DATABASE_CONNECT_ARGS ssl values and removed sslmode from the engine URL query.
Preserved production missing-DATABASE_URL startup fault behavior while applying asyncpg driver sanitation.

Verified all three supported connection string prefixes with sslmode=require resolve to SQLAlchemy drivername postgresql+asyncpg, empty URL query, and DATABASE_CONNECT_ARGS {'ssl': 'require'} in isolated Python imports.
Attempted local git commit for infra/database.py and agent_memory_harness.json, but .git/index.lock creation failed because .git is read-only in the sandbox.
